output: Executed batch 4 task `T7`. The provider now exposes the drag coordinator and indicator refs through context, `TimelineEditor` mounts `<DropIndicator>` and captures the live edit-area DOM node, `useClipDrag` now receives the shared coordinator handle, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and a checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/video-editor-timeline-well-20260325-1848/execution_batch_4.json`.
deviations: T7 required a small compatibility edit in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts` so the hook accepts the shared `coordinator` prop now, even though the actual migration to coordinator-driven drag behavior remains scheduled for T8.

task_updates.executor_notes: `useTimelineState` now retains the `useDragCoordinator(...)` result and exposes `coordinator`, `indicatorRef`, and `editAreaRef` through `TimelineEditorContextValue`. `TimelineEditor` consumes those refs, populates `editAreaRef.current` from `.timeline-editor-edit-area` in a layout effect, renders `<DropIndicator ref={indicatorRef} editAreaRef={editAreaRef} />` alongside `<Timeline>`, and passes `coordinator` into `useClipDrag`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `TimelineEditor` now reads `indicatorRef`, `editAreaRef`, and `coordinator` from context, mounts `<DropIndicator ref={indicatorRef} editAreaRef={editAreaRef} />`, and writes the live `.timeline-editor-edit-area` element into `editAreaRef.current`. The shared `coordinator` is present in `TimelineEditorContextValue`, so both `useClipDrag` and the future external-drop wiring can consume the same provider-level coordinator.
